prompt: Wide architectural low-angle interior view of a finished coffered ceiling spanning a 1980s New England colonial dining room — the ceiling fills the upper two-thirds of the frame. Six recessed coffer boxes in clean white-painted maple millwork, divided by warm-stained red oak beams, each coffer trimmed with stacked crown and panel-mold. A simple matte-black drum chandelier hangs centered in one of the middle coffer panels. The frame catches the top of the room: chair rail, dark crown line of a doorway, a hint of warm-painted wall. Shot with a 24mm wide architectural lens, late afternoon golden-hour sun streams through a window-side direction creating long warm shadow lines across the coffer profiles. Slightly desaturated palette, a brand-red Anjo-style mug edge in the lower frame as the only saturated color. NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
